sandbox_first_interpretation: MetaBlooms must prefer proven in-sandbox evidence paths for Android-phone workflows, while emitting PC/CI addenda as enhancement lanes rather than blockers.

sources.claim: Playwright uses managed browser installs such as `playwright install` and can install named browsers such as chromium/webkit/firefox; sandbox policy must treat this as unavailable unless the managed browser already exists or install network succeeds.
Chrome headless supports command-line screenshots via `--headless --screenshot --window-size=...`, so system Chromium is a valid attempted resolver method when available.
WeasyPrint is an HTML/CSS rendering engine that exports to PDF; it is valid as a render proxy but not as proof of browser JavaScript interaction.
WeasyPrint can read normal files and URLs and render HTML/CSS to PDF; sandbox-first resolver can use local file rendering without browser install.
